prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: One writer I believe is under-discussed is Flann O'Brien (real name Brian O'Nolan). He was an Irish novelist, playwright and journalist most active in the mid-20th century. His works were wildly inventive and often blended satire, absurdism and fantasy. O'Brien's novels like "At Swim-Two-Birds" and "The Third Policeman" were highly original and influential in terms of technique and style, but he has not received the same level of mainstream recognition as contemporaries like William Faulkner or James Joyce. His unique form of comic metafiction has a lot of untapped potential for discussion and appreciation.
